tranche: UP11-4 T1 retry with known-working model

in_scope: T1 retry with smaller known-working model on same harness

cp2_live_pod_proof.gpu_type: NVIDIA H100 80GB HBM3 (SXM, AP-IN-1 Secure Cloud)

cp4_local_env_wiring.env_file_updated: .env (RUNPOD_OPENAI_BASE_URL added on its own line; prior malformed-append bug that glued it onto HF_TOKEN was repaired truthfully)
cp4_local_env_wiring.runner_code_heavy_profile_materialized: .amof/runpod-profiles/runner_code_heavy.yaml copied from qwen25-coder-32b.yaml so the canonical profile id points at the proven serving model (operator choice, not plan-level change)

cp5_heavy_lane_health_proof.ui_smoke: The /runpod page served 200 and its hook calls the same /heavy-lane/health endpoint; post-flip it displays status=usable with zero missing prerequisites.

bugs_found_and_fixed_during_proof.bug: UP11-3 -> UP11-4 continuation: driver waited on RunPod REST portMappings to populate; the field stays null even when /v1/models serves 200. Driver would never probe.
bugs_found_and_fixed_during_proof.fix: runpod_t1_drive.py _resolve_openai_base_url falls back to the profile-declared http port when REST port_mappings is empty. Single-file 15-line change.
bugs_found_and_fixed_during_proof.proof: On UP11-4 attempt 2, the same proxy URL pattern hit 200; the driver's blind spot is what kept it idle for 25 minutes in attempt 3.

bugs_found_and_fixed_during_proof.bug: Driver did not interpolate ${VAR} references in profile env map from host env; HF_TOKEN injection required a risky plaintext value in the YAML.
bugs_found_and_fixed_during_proof.fix: runpod_t1_drive.py _interpolate_env_refs resolves ${VAR} from os.environ at pod creation time; profile YAML stays with HF_TOKEN: "${HF_TOKEN}".
bugs_found_and_fixed_during_proof.proof: Final pod 2r1oqntmc9bs78 had HF_TOKEN injected (len=37 from .env).

bugs_found_and_fixed_during_proof.bug: evaluate_heavy_lane_status required account.endpoint_count>0 (a RunPod Serverless endpoint) even when a pod-based endpoint serves /v1/models correctly. This prevented heavy-lane health from ever flipping to usable under a pod-based lane.
bugs_found_and_fixed_during_proof.fix: runpod_heavy_lane.py: only add runpod_openai_endpoint to missing when endpoint_count==0 AND amof_managed_pod_count==0. Added reason amof_managed_pod_present when a managed pod is seen.
bugs_found_and_fixed_during_proof.test_coverage: New test_pod_based_lane_is_usable_without_serverless_endpoint; existing tests unchanged (92 tests pass).

bugs_found_and_fixed_during_proof.bug: .env append without trailing newline on prior file glued RUNPOD_OPENAI_BASE_URL onto HF_TOKEN value; token was malformed on the earlier attempt (attempt 3) which explains ~25 min rate-limited download.
bugs_found_and_fixed_during_proof.fix: Idempotent rewrite of .env using Python that splits the two lines cleanly and ends the file with a newline. No secret logged.

cp6_next_proof_gate.recommendation: planning-only proof (call the heavy-lane once from the AMOF agent_runner strict path to confirm routing guardrails fire in the happy path with a real served model)
cp6_next_proof_gate.justification: Heavy lane is usable end-to-end; the next AMOF-side question is 'does the agent_runner pick this up without breaking its refusal contract?' rather than any further RunPod substrate work.

cost_summary.cap_status: not hit; pod stopped promptly after proof

mutations_performed: created + deleted pod 2r1oqntmc9bs78 (the one that proved T1)
three earlier qwen25-coder-32b attempt pods (created, manually stopped due to network drop / wrong HF_TOKEN / timing diagnosis)
two code fixes in repos/amof: runpod_t1_drive.py (port fallback + ${VAR} interp) and runpod_heavy_lane.py (pod-based usable path); +1 new test; 92 tests pass
kubectl set env on local controlplane for RUNPOD_OPENAI_BASE_URL
kubectl rollout restart on controlplane to pick up heavy-lane fix
